audit_status.source_base: Greek Acts text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Antioch prophets/teachers, Spirit sending, Cyprus, Elymas, Sergius Paulus, Pisidian Antioch synagogue speech, Davidic promise, resurrection, release/justification, Gentile joy, opposition.

verses.restored: Now there were in Antioch, in the assembly there, prophets and teachers: Barnabas, Simeon called Niger, Lucius of Cyrene, Manaen foster-brother of Herod the tetrarch, and Saul.
As they were serving the Lord and fasting, the holy Spirit said, Set apart for me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them.
Then fasting and praying and laying hands on them, they released them.
They therefore, being sent out by the holy Spirit, went down to Seleucia, and from there sailed away to Cyprus.
Coming to Salamis, they were announcing the word of the Divine in the synagogues of the Judeans. They also had John as assistant.
Having gone through the whole island as far as Paphos, they found a certain man, a magician, a false prophet, a Judean whose name was Bar-Jesus.
He was with the proconsul Sergius Paulus, an intelligent man. This one summoned Barnabas and Saul and sought to hear the word of the Divine.
But Elymas the magician, for thus his name is translated, opposed them, seeking to turn the proconsul away from the trust.
Saul, also called Paul, filled with holy Spirit, looked intently at him
and said, O full of all deceit and all recklessness, son of the adversarial one, enemy of all righteousness/justice, will you not stop twisting the straight ways of the Lord?

Now look, the hand of the Lord is upon you, and you will be blind, not seeing the sun for a season. Immediately mist and darkness fell upon him, and going around, he sought someone to lead him by the hand.
Then the proconsul, seeing what had happened, trusted, being astonished at the teaching of the Lord.
Putting out to sea from Paphos, Paul and those around him came to Perga of Pamphylia. John departed from them and returned to Jerusalem.
They, passing through from Perga, came to Antioch of Pisidia, and going into the synagogue on the Sabbath day, they sat down.
After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the synagogue rulers sent to them, saying, Men, siblings, if there is among you a word of encouragement for the people, speak.
Paul stood, motioned with the hand, and said, Men of Israel and those revering the Divine, listen.
The Divine of this people Israel chose our fathers, exalted the people in the sojourn in Egypt, and with uplifted arm led them out from it.
For about forty years he bore with them in the wilderness.
Having destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan, he gave them their land as inheritance.
After these things, about four hundred fifty years, he gave judges until Samuel the prophet.
Then they asked for a king, and the Divine gave them Saul son of Kish, a man from the tribe of Benjamin, for forty years.
Removing him, he raised up David for them as king, to whom also he bore witness, saying, I found David son of Jesse, a man according to my heart, who will do all my wills.
From this one's seed, according to promise, the Divine brought to Israel a Savior/Healer, Jesus.
Before his entrance, John had proclaimed a baptism of turning/realignment to all the people of Israel.
As John was completing the course, he said, What do you suppose me to be? I am not he. But look, one comes after me, of whose feet's sandal I am not worthy to loose.
Men, siblings, sons of Abraham's family and those among you revering the Divine, to us the word of this salvation/healing has been sent.
For those dwelling in Jerusalem and their rulers, not knowing this one nor the voices of the prophets read every Sabbath, fulfilled them by judging him.
Though finding no cause of death, they asked Pilate to have him killed.
When they completed all things written concerning him, taking him down from the tree, they placed him in a tomb.
But the Divine raised him from the dead.
He appeared for many days to those who had come up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem, who are now his witnesses to the people.
And we announce good news to you: the promise made to the fathers,
that the Divine has fulfilled this to us, their children, by raising Jesus, as also it is written in the second Psalm: You are my Son; today I have begotten you.
That he raised him from the dead, no longer about to return to decay, he has spoken thus: I will give you the holy and faithful things of David.
Therefore also in another place he says, You will not give your Holy One to see decay.
For David, after serving the purpose of the Divine in his own generation, fell asleep and was placed with his fathers and saw decay.
But the one whom the Divine raised did not see decay.
Therefore let it be known to you, men, siblings, that through this one release of sins/misalignments is announced to you.
And from all things from which you were not able to be made righteous/just in the Law of Moses, in this one everyone trusting is made righteous/just.
Therefore watch, lest what has been spoken in the Prophets come upon you:
Look, you despisers, marvel and vanish, because I work a work in your days, a work you would certainly not trust if someone described it to you.
As they were going out, they were urging that these words be spoken to them on the next Sabbath.
When the synagogue gathering was dismissed, many of the Judeans and reverent proselytes followed Paul and Barnabas, who spoke to them and were persuading them to remain in the grace of the Divine.
On the coming Sabbath almost the whole city gathered to hear the word of the Lord.
But the Judeans, seeing the crowds, were filled with jealousy and were contradicting the things spoken by Paul, blaspheming.

Paul and Barnabas spoke boldly and said, It was necessary for the word of the Divine to be spoken first to you. Since you push it away and judge yourselves unworthy of age-long life, look, we turn to the nations.
For so the Lord has commanded us: I have set you as light for nations, that you may be for salvation/healing as far as the end of the earth.
Hearing this, the nations were rejoicing and glorifying the word of the Lord, and as many as were appointed to age-long life trusted.
The word of the Lord was being carried through the whole region.
But the Judeans stirred up the reverent prominent women and the first men of the city, and raised persecution against Paul and Barnabas, and drove them out from their borders.
They shook off the dust from their feet against them and came into Iconium.
And the disciples were filled with joy and holy Spirit.
